# hitloop.agency — Bryan Balli

> Bryan Balli is an AI design engineer and creative technologist building AI-assisted client intelligence platforms, high-performance web experiences, and modular intake pipelines. Based in Chicago. Available for consulting engagements worldwide.

Bryan Balli works at the intersection of front-end engineering (Next.js, Three.js, GSAP) and applied AI (Claude API, OpenAI). He builds AI-assisted systems that compress discovery, briefing, and audit workflows into automated pipelines — replacing manual spreadsheet-and-email loops with structured, actionable intelligence delivered to a client dashboard.

## Public pages

- [Homepage](https://hitloop.agency/): Portfolio, featured work, testimonials from TikTok, HBO Max, Epsilon, and TST. Agency marquee (Publicis, Epsilon, Conversant, Alliance Data).
- [About](https://hitloop.agency/about): Background, methodology, and experience spanning Chicago, San Francisco, and remote teams.
- [Work](https://hitloop.agency/work): Selected projects — AI-assisted platforms, Three.js interactive experiences, design systems, SEO/GEO intelligence.
- [Case Studies](https://hitloop.agency/case-studies): Process documentation and results — from intake through delivery.
- [FAQ](https://hitloop.agency/faq): Common questions about AI design engineering, creative technology, engagement structure, pricing ($3,500 starting scope), timelines (2–6 weeks), and the intake pipeline.
- [How It Works](https://hitloop.agency/how-it-works): The three-phase process — intake brief, scope and prototype, build and handoff.
- [Process](https://hitloop.agency/process): Phased, approval-gated workflow from discovery through handoff.
- [Gallery](https://hitloop.agency/gallery): Visual samples across web, motion, brand, and interactive.
- [Contact](https://hitloop.agency/contact): Book a 30-minute call or start with the async intake brief.

## Services

- [AI Design Consulting](https://hitloop.agency/services/ai-design-consulting): AI-assisted workflows, client intelligence pipelines, and production-quality web systems.
- [Brand Identity](https://hitloop.agency/services/brand-identity): Design system extraction, brand snapshot analysis, and visual identity work.
- [Design Systems](https://hitloop.agency/services/design-systems): Component libraries, token architecture, and design-to-code pipelines.
- [SEO & GEO](https://hitloop.agency/services/seo-geo): AI-assisted SEO audits, Core Web Vitals analysis, and generative engine optimization for AI Overviews, ChatGPT, and Perplexity.
- [Web Development](https://hitloop.agency/services/web-development): High-performance builds using Next.js, React, GSAP, and Three.js.

## Gated product surfaces (require sign-in — not publicly crawlable)

- [Dashboard](/dashboard): Client workspace running the modular card chain (Multi-Device View → Social Preview → Brand Snapshot → SEO Performance).
- [Auth](/auth): Firebase Auth sign-in and signup.

## Modular card pipeline

- **Multi-Device View** — Captures desktop / tablet / mobile screenshots via Browserless, composites a device-frame mockup with sharp, and stores full-page screenshots in Firebase Storage. Supports split retry paths (mockup-only rebuild from existing viewport captures, or full-page-only recapture).
- **Social Preview** — Fetches the site's HTML, parses metadata (title, description, image, favicon, theme color, canonical, site name). Flags missing fields with a human-readable lead-with-what's-wrong description.
- **Brand Snapshot (style-guide)** — Collects HTML + external stylesheets, extracts colors / typography / layout / motion via Anthropic's API, then deterministically re-anchors the heading font to the actual `h1` selector's `font-family` so the extraction can't pick a menu/nav font by mistake.
- **SEO + Performance Snapshot** — Runs PageSpeed Insights v5 and a separate AI SEO audit in parallel. The data-driven description leads with the dominant finding (AI visibility gap, critical issue, or clean scores) and lists the scores that are in reasonable shape.

## Technical substrate

- Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack build)
- React 19 client components
- Firebase Admin (Auth, Firestore, Storage) for persistence
- Anthropic API for design system extraction and card scribe
- Browserless for headless screenshot capture
- PageSpeed Insights v5 + custom `ai-seo-audit` engine
- GSAP (ScrollTrigger, Flip) for marketing-surface scroll animations

## Conventions for AI summarization

- "Cross-Device Layouts" and "Multi-Device View" are the same card.
- "Brand Snapshot" and "style-guide" refer to the same module.
- "STATUS: Passed" means every required artifact for a card is present in `dashboard_state`. "STATUS: NEEDS ATTENTION" is the unified non-passing label for any card with partial or critical findings.
- "Chain" refers to the ordered unlock sequence: `multi-device-view → social-preview → style-guide → seo-performance`. Each card unlocks the next once it has been run (succeeded, partial, or failed are all progress).
